sections.heading: Introduction
sections.content: There are five scope functions in Kotlin: let, run, with, apply, and also. They all execute a block of code on an object but differ in how the object is referenced and what they return.

sections.heading: 1. apply (The Configuration Tool)
sections.content: Context Object: this | Return Value: The object itself. Use apply when you want to initialize or configure an object. It is commonly used in Android for setting up objects like Intents or UI properties.

sections.heading: 2. let (The Null-Safety Tool)
sections.content: Context Object: it | Return Value: Lambda result. let is primarily used to execute a block only if an object is not null, or to transform an object into a different result.

sections.heading: 3. also (The Side-Effect Tool)
sections.content: Context Object: it | Return Value: The object itself. Use also when you want to perform an additional action that doesn't modify the object, such as logging or validation.

sections.heading: 4. with (The Grouping Tool)
sections.content: Context Object: this | Return Value: Lambda result. with is not an extension function; it takes the object as an argument. Use it when you want to call multiple methods on the same object without repeating its name.

sections.heading: 5. run (The Hybrid Tool)
sections.content: Context Object: this | Return Value: Lambda result. run combines object configuration and result computation.

sections.heading: Summary
items: Scope functions create a temporary scope for an object to improve readability.
apply and also return the object itself (useful for chaining).
let, run, and with return the last expression of the block.
In Android, apply is commonly used for UI setup.
let is commonly used for null safety.
